summary: FedP3 studies heterogeneous federated learning under resource constraints, using personalized model pruning so clients with different compute and bandwidth budgets can train and communicate more efficiently while retaining model quality.
title: FedP3: Heterogeneous Federated Learning with Personalized Neural Network Pruning

summary: This paper targets federated learning under intertwined data and device heterogeneity, proposing a system-oriented approach that allocates adaptive model capacity so clients with different hardware limits and data characteristics can participate effectively.
title: Tackling Intertwined Data and Device Heterogeneities in Federated Learning via Adaptive Dual Side Depth

summary: Distributed Pruning looks at how to train compact neural networks in federated settings, emphasizing communication and device-efficiency tradeoffs that matter when clients have limited resources.
title: Distributed Pruning Towards Tiny Neural Networks in Federated Learning

summary: This work studies hierarchical federated learning for scalability and non-IID robustness, using a label-driven knowledge aggregation design to coordinate learning across multiple levels rather than a single flat server-client topology.
title: Towards Scalable and Non-IID Robust Hierarchical Federated Learning via Label-Driven Knowledge Aggregator
